{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "R1.4": 3, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.3": "The critique identifies potential issues with claim-evidence linkage regarding the novelty and accuracy claims.", "R1.4": "The critique mentions prior work (DrugDock-v1) but doesn't delve into a comparative analysis against multiple other methods.", "RX.1": "The critique focuses on substantive issues rather than expressing uncertainty about its own assessment.", "RX.3": "The critique acknowledges the limited scope of the evaluation (three target classes) and suggests expanding it.", "RX.4": "The critique highlights potential harm in overstating the model's capabilities and lack of external validation.", "RX.5": "The critique doesn't discuss the provenance or traceability of the information presented."},
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.8, "RX.4": 0.8, "RX.5": 0.4},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The critique identifies potential issues with claim-evidence linkage regarding the novelty and accuracy claims.
rationale.R1.4: The critique mentions prior work (DrugDock-v1) but doesn't delve into a comparative analysis against multiple other methods.
rationale.RX.1: The critique focuses on substantive issues rather than expressing uncertainty about its own assessment.
rationale.RX.3: The critique acknowledges the limited scope of the evaluation (three target classes) and suggests expanding it.
rationale.RX.4: The critique highlights potential harm in overstating the model's capabilities and lack of external validation.
rationale.RX.5: The critique doesn't discuss the provenance or traceability of the information presented.
